title: roettlera pygmaea
intro: Roettlera pygmaea, also known as Dwarf St. John's Wort, is a species of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, Hypericaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat depression, anxiety, and insomnia due to its high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have antidepressant effects. The plant's health benefits also include its antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Roettlera pygmaea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and improve mood.
